subnetworks.blurb: The World Genome Academy builds genomics education and research in the open, under a five-council model — Education, Agriculture, Research, an Indigenous-data veto, and Technology — so the science is stewarded, not enclosed.
subnetworks.ethos_alignment: Scientific knowledge and education, held in common
subnetworks.tagline: Genomics knowledge as a shared commons

subnetworks.blurb: A community program for agentic-context literacy and enablement — and the shared pool that forms when vaults publish their best context, patterns, and lattices for others to discover and reuse.
subnetworks.ethos_alignment: Context as a commons people contribute to and steward

subnetworks.blurb: The Wilhelm AI Initiative for the Undiagnosed (AI4U) — the Wilhelm Foundation's umbrella over AI initiatives for people and families living with undiagnosed disease, including the open Rare-AI Archive.
subnetworks.ethos_alignment: Rare-disease AI as a public good
subnetworks.serves: People & families living with undiagnosed disease
subnetworks.tagline: AI for people living with undiagnosed disease

subnetworks.blurb: Rare Archive is an open-source rare-disease AI project — the diagnosis-acceleration pillar of AI4U — governed by a five-seat steward council, with a canonical home at Wilhelm-Foundation/rare-archive.
subnetworks.ethos_alignment: Rare-disease AI built in the open
subnetworks.steward: Mnemosyne · five-seat steward council · anchor Wilhelm Foundation
